shape.ParameterDeclaration: fn.getParameters() → ParameterDeclaration[]; p.getName(): string; p.getType(): Type
shape.key_api_note: ts-morph is used for parameter name extraction. ts-json-schema-generator is used for JSON Schema generation from TS types. Do NOT use ts-morph to generate JSON Schema — it produces shallow shapes.

ts-json-schema-generator.interface: createGenerator({ path, type, tsconfig? }).createSchema(typeName?: string): JSONSchema

shape.createGenerator: createGenerator({ path: absolutePath, type: typeName, tsconfig? }) → SchemaGenerator
shape.config: { path, type, tsconfig, skipTypeCheck?, additionalProperties?, encodeRefs? }
shape.key_api_note: Pass the TypeScript TYPE NAME as `type`, not the parameter name. For a param `userId: string`, the type is 'string'. For a param `data: UserInput`, the type is 'UserInput'. The morph-fallback is triggered when createGenerator throws (usually for inline/anonymous types).

mcp-sdk.interface: new Server({name,version},{capabilities:{tools:{}}}); server.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema|CallToolRequestSchema, handler); await server.connect(new StdioServerTransport() | new SSEServerTransport(path,res) | new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({sessionIdGenerator?}))

shape.Server: new Server({ name, version }, { capabilities: { tools: {} } })
shape.SSEServerTransport: new SSEServerTransport(path, res) — requires raw http.ServerResponse
shape.tool_shape: { name: string, description: string, inputSchema: JSONSchema }
shape.CallToolRequest: { params: { name: string, arguments: Record<string, unknown> } }
shape.key_api_note: SDK ships all three transports. For stdio: server.connect(new StdioServerTransport()). For HTTP/SSE: create a node:http server, route requests to transport.handleRequest(req, res), then server.connect(transport). No express dependency needed.

nx-devkit.interface: addProjectConfiguration(tree, name, config); generateFiles(tree, templateDir, targetDir, subs); updateJson(tree, path, fn); await formatFiles(tree); names(raw): {name,className,propertyName,constantName,fileName}

shape.Tree: Virtual file system for generators. tree.exists(path), tree.read(path, encoding), tree.write(path, content), tree.delete(path)
shape.generateFiles: generateFiles(tree, templateDir, targetDir, substitutions: Record<string, string>)
shape.formatFiles: await formatFiles(tree) — runs prettier on all modified files
shape.names: names(rawName) → { name, className, propertyName, constantName, fileName }
shape.ExecutorContext: { root: string, projectName: string, workspace: { projects: Record<string, ProjectConfig> } }
shape.key_api_note: Templates in __files__/ use EJS-style <%=variable%> for substitutions. File extension __tmpl__ is stripped. formatFiles must be awaited.

shape.option_repeatable: .option('--opt <kv>', desc, (val, prev) => [...prev, val], [] as string[]) — accumulator pattern for --opt key=value
shape.action: .action(async (opts) => { ... }) — opts is the parsed options object
shape.parseAsync: program.parseAsync() — async-safe; use instead of parse()
shape.key_api_note: Use .option() not .requiredOption() for optional flags. Boolean flags: .option('--flag') with no <value> placeholder.

fastify.interface: const app = Fastify(); app.post<{Body:T}>(path, async (req,reply) => value); await app.listen({port,host?}); await app.close()

shape.Fastify: import Fastify from 'fastify'; const app = Fastify()
shape.app.post: app.post<{Body: T}>(path, async (req, reply) => { ... return value }) — return value is JSON-serialized
shape.key_api_note: Do NOT add { schema: { body: ... } } to route options — AJV rejects complex oneOf/anyOf from ts-json-schema-generator. Body validation is deferred to dispatch().

express.interface: const app = express(); app.use(express.json()); const r = Router(); r.post(path, async (req,res) => res.json(result)); const server = app.listen(port); server.close()

shape.express: import express from 'express'; const app = express()
shape.express.json(): app.use(express.json()) — must be added before routes
shape.Router: import { Router } from 'express'; const router = Router()
shape.router.post: router.post(path, async (req, res) => { const result = ...; res.json(result) })
shape.key_api_note: Unlike Fastify, Express handler must call res.json() not return the value. Use supertest for integration testing.
